title: How I Built ArtResin: From Headaches in My Studio to a Global Brand
desc: David Zak tells the founding story of ArtResin — how frustration with toxic, yellowing epoxy led him to partner with chemists and build an artist-safe resin brand sold in over 20 countries.

title: Motion Design for the World's Biggest Brands
desc: David Zak on 17+ years of motion design and animation for Pepsi, Coca-Cola, Samsung, BlackBerry, NASCAR, FedEx and major banks — and what working through top agencies really teaches you.

title: Exiting the Company I Founded: What the 2024 ArtResin Buyout Taught Me
desc: David Zak reflects on exiting ArtResin in 2024 via partner buyout — what it feels like to leave a company you founded, and what comes after the exit.

title: How We Private-Labeled Butane Torches (and Why Accessories Win in E-Commerce)
desc: David Zak on ArtResin's private-label butane torch line — sourcing, packaging, and the accessory strategy that grows an e-commerce brand beyond its hero product.

title: LiDAR Scanning Fast Food Restaurants: My Strangest Freelance Niche
desc: David Zak on his unexpected freelance specialty — 3D LiDAR spatial scanning of Tim Hortons, Burger King, Popeyes, Taco Bell and Peet's Coffee locations.

title: Why I Went Back to Client Work After Selling My Company
desc: After exiting ArtResin in 2024, David Zak returned to motion design and creative direction. Here's why a founder goes back to client work — and what changes when he does.
